titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Photographs of Migrant Agricultural Laborers in California
Photographs of Migrant Agricultural Laborers in California
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 28, 2024 at 00:48

did.unittitle: Photographs of Migrant Agricultural Laborers in California

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: 4 boxes
did.abstract: Collection of thirty-eight photographs that document aspects of housing, medical care, and education for migrant agricultural laborers in California, 1969-1971, created by photographers Harvey Saul Columbus and Calind Lewis Potts as well as by the California Farm Workers Health Service. Many of the agricultural laborers are Hispanic Americans as well as several African Americans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection of thirty-eight photographs that document aspects of housing, medical care, and education for migrant agricultural laborers in California, 1969-1971, created by photographers Harvey Saul Columbus and Calind Lewis Potts as well as by the California Farm Workers Health Service. Many of the agricultural laborers are Hispanic Americans as well as several African Americans.

Seven photographs by Calind Lewis Potts document education, including classroom scenes, as well as housing for migrant agricultural laborers at Dixon, Empire, Hollister, Lodi, and Merced in California, approximately 1970.

Photographs by Harvey Saul Columbus include twelve photographs that chiefly document a free medical clinic at Livingston, California, operated by the Stanford University School of Medicine including student F. Wells Shoemaker and faculty member Keith Breden Taylor in 1969. Seventeen other photographs by Columbus document medical clinics and housing for migrant agricultural laborers at Calexico, Fresno, Imperial, Lodi, Mace, Mendota, San Joaquin County, Stockton, Sutter County, and Watsonville, approximately 1970.

Two photographs by the California Farm Workers Health Service document a woman at a grape packing factory as well as agricultural workers harvesting tomatoes at unidentified locations, approximately 1970.
Manuscript inscriptions on rectos and versos of mounts. Titles for prints in this guide derive from inscriptions on rectos and versos of mounts.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from the William Reese Co. on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2022.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Photographs of Migrant Agricultural Laborers in California collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Photographs of Migrant Agricultural Laborers in California. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, as well as more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This guide derives from a preliminary listing by Ashley Cale based on a transcription from inscriptions on prints followed by detailed examination of each print, arrangement, and description by Matthew Daniel Mason.

bioghist.head: Harvey Saul Columbus (born 1942)
bioghist.p: Harvey Saul Columbus, also known as Sol Columbus, was a photographer. Born in Michigan and a son of Ida Barbara Steiner Columbus (1915-1961) and Benjamin Columbus (1912-1996). In January 1984 Columbus married Linda Ann Koester (born 1949). He lived in Novato and San Francisco, California.

bioghist.head: Calind Lewis Potts (1920-2016)
bioghist.p: Calind Lewis Potts, also known as Cal Potts, was a photographer, teacher, and construction inspector. He was born in Indiana, a son of Walter Earl Potts (1889-1953) and Effie May Handley Potts (1890-1987). Potts was employed at the Plano Cabinet & Fixture Company in Porterville, California, during the 1960s. Potts then worked with Migrant Services Programs in the California Office of Economic Opportunity, approximately 1970, as well as with the Migrant Services Section of the Division of Farm Labor Services for the California State Department of Human Resources, approximately 1980-1981. He was married to Annette Rosemond Potts (1920-2008), and they had two children.

bioghist.head: California Farm Workers Health Service
bioghist.p: The California Farm Workers Health Service was a program established by the California State Department of Health in 1961 that included decentralized medical clinics to serve migrant agricultural laborers and their families during the peak harvest months.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Photographs by Calind Lewis Potts, approximately 1970. II. Photographs by Harvey Saul Columbus, 1969-1971. III. Photographs by the California Farm Workers Health Service, approximately 1970.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs by Calind Lewis Potts that document education, including classroom scenes, as well as housing for migrant agricultural laborers at Dixon, Empire, Hollister, Lodi, and Merced in California, approximately 1970.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into five subseries: Dixon, Empire, Hollister, Lodi, and Merced.

did.physdesc: 7 photographic prints, 34.3 x 34.8 cm and smaller, on mounts, 50.7 x 40.7 cm

title.part: Waiting for the School Bus, Office of Economic Opportunity, Migrant Labor Camp at Dixon

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 22.5 x 34.5 cm, on mount, 50.7 x 40.7 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior view of a large group of children as well as a few adults outside a building; inscription of title on recto of mount

title.part: Recess, Empire Migrant Labor Camp, Office of Economic Opportunity

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 26.2 x 35.1 cm, on mount, 50.7 x 40.7 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior view of a playground with two women and fourteen children among two sandboxes; inscription of title on recto of mount

did.unittitle: Interior view of a woman and seven children listening to a record player
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 26.8 x 34.5 cm, on mount, 50.7 x 40.7 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Untitled photograph; inscription on verso: Cal Potts, Hollister

did.unittitle: Interior view of a woman reading a book to ten children in a classroom
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 26.1 x 34.4 cm, on mount, 50.7 x 40.7 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Untitled photograph; inscription on verso: Cal Potts, Hollister

title.part: Volunteer Help with the Refrigerators. Office of Economic Opportunity, Migrant Labor Camp at Hollister

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 34.3 x 25.8 cm, on mount, 50.7 x 40.7 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior view of four boys assisting a man and a partially visible woman move a refrigerator into a building; inscription of title on recto of mount

title.part: Strictly Posed, Harney Lane Migrant Labor Camp, Office of Economic Opportunity

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 27.1 x 34.4 cm, on mount, 50.7 x 40.7 cm

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Harney Lane was a migrant agricultural laborers camp in Lodi, San Joaquin County, California.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior view of seven children sitting and standing outside a building

title.part: Roman Bath (Migrant in a Tub), Merced Migrant Labor Camp, Office of Economic Opportunity

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 27 x 34.8 cm, on mount, 50.7 x 40.7 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior view of two children, one sitting in a wash tub; inscription of title on recto of mount; sheet affixed to recto with alternate title: Bath Time at Merced Labor Camp

did.physdesc: 29 photographic prints, 23.7 x 24 cm and smaller, on mounts, 25.7 x 25.6 cm and smaller, on mounts, 50.9 x 40.5 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs by Harvey Saul Columbus that chiefly document a free medical clinic at Livingston, California, operated by the Stanford University School of Medicine including student F. Wells Shoemaker and faculty member Keith Breden Taylor in 1969. Other photographs by Columbus document medical clinics and housing for migrant agricultural laborers at Calexico, Fresno, Imperial, Lodi, Mace, Mendota, San Joaquin County, Stockton, Sutter County, and Watsonville, 1969-1971. Photographs are mounted on double-mounts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into eleven subseries: Calexico, Fresno, Imperial, Livingston, Lodi, Mace, Mendota, San Joaquin County, Stockton, Sutter County, and Watsonville.

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 18.6 x 23.8 cm, on mount, 20.4 x 25.6 cm, on mount, 50.9 x 40.5 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interior view of a room with cots and lockers

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 23.7 x 17.3 cm, on mount, 25.1 x 18.1 cm, on mount, 50.9 x 40.5 cm
1 photographic print, 18.7 x 23.7 cm, on mount, 20.2 x 25.4 cm, on mount, 50.9 x 40.5 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interior view of a diner that includes seven men seated a bar and another standing as well as two men serving behind the bar

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 18.4 x 23.7 cm, on mount, 20.4 x 25.4 cm, on mount, 50.9 x 40.5 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior view of three men at the 900-block of Fresno, California, with the Mission Studio operated by photographer Ezequiel Chequele Guzman in the background

title.part: Micaela Sandoval, Widow, Imperial City, California

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 18.6 x 17.3 cm, on mount, 21.3 x 18.7 cm, on mount, 50.9 x 40.5 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior portrait of Micaela Sandoval with her arm raised

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: From June through October 1969, F. Wells (Franklin Wells) Shoemaker (born 1946), a student in the Stanford University School of Medicine, and Dr. A. Frank (Adolph Frank) Brewer (1903-1977), the public health officer for Merced County, established a free medical clinic for migrant agricultural laborers and low income families at Livingston, California. Other students involved with the clinic include Larry Crapo, Han J. Dankers, Joe Neal, and Patrick Sullivan. The clinic also had bilingual aides trained in nursing and community work with their salaries paid by the Office of Economic Opportunity. One of the faculty members involved at this stage was Dr. Keith Breden Taylor (1924-2007), the George de Forest Barnett Professor of Medicine in the Medical School at Stanford University from 1963 to 1989. In 1970, the Stanford Medical School established a community health clinic operated by faculty and students.

title.part: Dr. Keith Taylor and Stanford medical students examine patient, Livingston Migrant Clinic

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 18.2 x 22.8 cm, on mount, 20.2 x 24.2 cm, on mount, 50.9 x 40.5 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interior view of F. Wells (Franklin Wells) Shoemaker (born 1946) examining a male patient with Dr. Keith Breden Taylor (1924-2007) in center and two other medical students

title.part: Dr. Wolohan talks to woman patient, Livingston Medical Project.

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 18.5 x 21.7 cm, on mount, 20.4 x 25.5 cm, on mount, 50.9 x 40.5 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interior view of Dr. Jeremiah John Wolohan (1917-1996) with a female patient

title.part: Harney Lane Night Clinic, Dr. Guey Mark

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 16.8 x 23.9 cm, on mount, 19.3 x 25.4 cm, on mount, 50.9 x 40.5 cm

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Harney Lane was a migrant agricultural laborers camp in Lodi, San Joaquin County, California.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interior view of Dr. Guey C. Mark (1928-2010) examining a boy with a girl and woman, presumably his mother and sister

title.part: Migrant Clinic, Mace Camp. University of California, Davis Project

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 18.7 x 23.1 cm, on mount, 20.4 x 25.4 cm, on mount, 50.9 x 40.5 cm

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mace Camp was a service site of the Migrant Workers Comprehensive Health program operated by the University of California, Davis, in 1971

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interior view of four women and one man sitting

title.part: Migrant Family, Coit Ranch. Mendota, California

did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 17.8 x 24 cm, on mount, 20.3 x 25.3 cm, on mount, 50.9 x 40.5 cm

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Frank Raymond Coit (1908-1970) operated the Coit Ranch and grew cantaloupes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior portrait of a boy, woman, and man, likely family members, at Coit Ranch, Mendota, California.

did.unittitle: Photographs by the California Farm Workers Health Service
did.physdesc: 2 photographic prints, 23.9 x 24 cm and smaller, on mounts 28.1 x 35.8 cm

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The California Farm Workers Health Service was a program established by the California State Department of Health in 1961 that included decentralized medical clinics to serve migrant agricultural laborers and their families during the peak harvest months.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs by the California Farm Workers Health Service document a woman at a grape packing factor as well as agricultural workers harvesting tomatoes at unidentified locations, approximately 1970.

did.unittitle: Exterior view of a woman at a packing factory, likely packing grapes
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 23.9 x 19.4 cm, on mount, 28.1 x 35.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Untitled photograph

did.unittitle: Exterior view of children and adults harvesting tomatoes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Untitled photograph; ink stamp on verso: State Department of Public Health, Farm Workers Health Service
